response.justification: The candidate does not meet the minimum qualifications for this role. The most critical disqualifier is C++ experience: the position requires 8 years, but the resume shows approximately 6 months of C++ work at National Instruments (2007–2008) plus a student thesis project, and C++ is absent from the skills section entirely. The candidate's primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. While there is genuine distributed-systems depth at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second) and broad infrastructure exposure across Kubernetes, AWS CDK, and CI/CD tooling, these do not compensate for the missing C++ foundation, the absence of cloud compute platform ownership, and the lack of a formal technical leadership track over cloud or DevOps engineers.
strengths: 20+ years of engineering experience including high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second with async I/O and low-latency design)
Broad infrastructure exposure across Kubernetes, AWS CDK, Docker, CI/CD, and event-driven architectures across multiple production roles
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing through workshops, conference talks, and code kata sessions, indicating mentoring aptitude
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient — roughly 6 months at National Instruments in 2007–2008 against an 8-year minimum requirement, and C++ does not appear in the skills section
No evidence of owning or leading a cloud compute platform; cloud exposure is incidental (AWS CDK at Optilyz, Kubernetes for an open-source side project) rather than platform-level leadership
Primary technical identity is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, which is fundamentally misaligned with the C++ and large-scale cloud compute infrastructure core of this role
